zh.Hempstead, Essex, England: 英國英格蘭艾可普斯亞德
zh.Woodhall Manor, Great Sampford, Essex, E: 英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 桑普福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas de Huntington III於1435年出生在英國艾可普斯亞德時，他的父親Walter de Huntington63歲，母親Margaret56歲。他於1458年與Margaret Tyrell在英國艾可普斯亞德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1498年12月28日在他的家鄉享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可普斯亞德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas de Huntington III于1435年出生在英国艾可普斯亞德时，他的父亲Walter de Huntington63岁，母亲Margaret56岁。他于1458年与Margaret Tyrell在英国艾可普斯亞德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1498年12月28日在他的家乡享年63岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可普斯亞德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Huntington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

variants.name: Hunt
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name for a hunter, from Middle English hunte ‘hunter, huntsman’ (Old English hunta). The term was used not only of the hunting on horseback of game such as stags and wild boars, which in the Middle Ages was a pursuit restricted to the ranks of the nobility, but also to much humbler forms of pursuit such as bird catching and poaching for food. The word seems also to have been used as an Old English personal name and to have survived into the Middle Ages as an occasional personal name. Compare Huntington and Huntley .
Irish: adopted for various Irish surnames containing or thought to contain the Gaelic element fiadhach ‘hunt’; for example Ó Fiaich (see Fee ) and Ó Fiachna (see Fenton ).
Possibly an Americanized form of German   Hundt  .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas de Huntington III was born in 1435, in Hempstead, Essex, England, his father, Walter de Huntington, was 63 and his mother, Margaret, was 56. He married Margaret Tyrell in 1458, in Hempstead, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 28 December 1498, in his hometown, at the age of 63, and was buried in Hempstead, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas de Huntington III est né en 1435, à Hempstead, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Walter de Huntington, avait 63 ans et sa mère, Margaret, avait 56 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Tyrell en 1458, à Hempstead, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 28 décembre 1498, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Hempstead, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Huntington IIIが1435年にイギリスエセックスヘンプステアドで生まれた時，父Walter de Huntingtonは63歳，母Margaretは56歳であった。1458年にイギリスエセックスヘンプステアドでMargaret Tyrellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1498年12月28日に故郷で63歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスヘンプステアドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Huntington III(이)가 1435년 영국 에세스 헤프스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter de Huntington(은)는 63세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret(은)는 56세였다. 그는 1458년 영국 에세스 헤프스테아드에서 Margaret Tyrell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1498년 12월 28일 그의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 헤프스테아드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas de Huntington III родился в 1435 г. в местности хемпстид, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Walter de Huntington было 63 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret, 56. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Tyrell в 1458 г. в местности хемпстид, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 28 декабря 1498 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности хемпстид, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
